ALLOW_MANAGE_SUMMARIES.all-resources: Grants broad access to ALL RESOURCES in the target scope.
ALLOW_MANAGE_SUMMARIES.bastion: Allows managing OCI Bastion resources (create/update/delete/move bastions and control session capability).
ALLOW_MANAGE_SUMMARIES.bastion-session: Allows managing OCI Bastion sessions (create/update/delete session access to target resources).
ALLOW_MANAGE_SUMMARIES.dynamic-groups: Allows managing dynamic-groups (editing matching rules can grant workloads/user-controlled resources powerful access).
ALLOW_MANAGE_SUMMARIES.groups: Allows managing groups (can enable privilege changes by controlling group membership / lifecycle).
ALLOW_MANAGE_SUMMARIES.instances: Allows managing Compute Instances in scope (e.g., modify/attach/move/create/delete depending on permissions).
ALLOW_MANAGE_SUMMARIES.policies: Allows managing policies (create/update policies to grant escalation paths).
ALLOW_MANAGE_SUMMARIES.secret-bundles: Allows managing secret bundles (often equivalent to secret material access depending on environment).
ALLOW_MANAGE_SUMMARIES.users: Allows managing users (high impact: password resets, API keys, auth tokens, etc.).

edge.description: Grants broad access to ALL RESOURCES in the target scope.
edge.label: OCI_MANAGE

edge.description: Allows managing Compute Instances in scope (e.g., modify/attach/move/create/delete depending on permissions).
edge.label: OCI_MANAGE

edge.description: Allows managing users (high impact: password resets, API keys, auth tokens, etc.).
edge.label: OCI_MANAGE

edge.description: Allows managing groups (can enable privilege changes by controlling group membership / lifecycle).
edge.label: OCI_MANAGE

edge.description: Allows managing dynamic-groups (editing matching rules can grant workloads/user-controlled resources powerful access).
edge.label: OCI_MANAGE

edge.description: Allows managing policies (create/update policies to grant escalation paths).
edge.label: OCI_MANAGE

edge.description: Allows managing secret bundles (often equivalent to secret material access depending on environment).
edge.label: OCI_MANAGE

edge.description: Allows managing OCI Bastion resources (create/update/delete/move bastions and control session capability).
edge.label: OCI_MANAGE

edge.description: Allows managing OCI Bastion sessions (create/update/delete session access to target resources).
edge.label: OCI_MANAGE

edge.description: Create an API key for another user (requires USER_UPDATE + USER_APIKEY_ADD and enables API access as that user).
edge.label: OCI_CREATE_USER_API_KEY

edge.description: Use tag namespaces in scope (TAG_NAMESPACE_USE; enables applying defined tags at resource creation/update time).
edge.label: OCI_USE_TAG_NAMESPACE

edge.description: Create IAM users in scope (persistence/pivot capability; follow-on credential rights are needed for direct user impersonation).
edge.label: OCI_CREATE_USER

edge.description: Create IAM policies in scope (can grant yourself/admin-equivalent permissions).
edge.label: OCI_CREATE_POLICY

edge.description: Launch compute instances in scope (instances prerequisite).
edge.label: OCI_CREATE_INSTANCE

edge.description: Launch compute instances in scope (VNIC prerequisites).
edge.label: OCI_CREATE_INSTANCE

edge.description: Launch compute instances in scope (subnet prerequisites).
edge.label: OCI_CREATE_INSTANCE

edge.description: Create/run compute instance-agent commands in scope (can execute arbitrary commands on target instances).
edge.label: OCI_CREATE_INSTANCE_AGENT_COMMAND

edge.description: Create/run compute instance-agent commands in scope (can execute arbitrary commands on target instances).
edge.label: OCI_CREATE_INSTANCE_AGENT_COMMAND

edge.description: Read Run Command input payloads in scope (command text/parameters can contain secrets/tokens).
edge.label: OCI_READ_RUN_INPUT

edge.description: Read Run Command input payloads in scope (command text/parameters can contain secrets/tokens).
edge.label: OCI_READ_RUN_INPUT

edge.description: Read Run Command output metadata/results in scope (plaintext output can expose credentials and tokens).
edge.label: OCI_READ_RUN_OUTPUT

edge.description: Read Run Command output metadata/results in scope (plaintext output can expose credentials and tokens).
edge.label: OCI_READ_RUN_OUTPUT

edge.description: Update IAM policies in scope (can modify existing policy statements to escalate).
edge.label: OCI_UPDATE_POLICY

edge.description: Update dynamic-group rules (can target attacker-controlled resources and inherit the group's permissions).
edge.label: OCI_UPDATE_DYNAMIC_GROUP_RULES

edge.description: Read secret material from secret bundles (passwords/API keys/tokens → lateral movement).
edge.label: OCI_DUMP_SECRETS

bastion.target.bastion-session.username: [String] Use this variable to control access to sessions by OS username.
bastion.target.bastion.name: [String] Use this variable to control access to bastions by bastion name.
bastion.target.bastion.ocid: [Entity (OCID)] Use this variable to control access to a specific bastion by OCID.
bastion.target.resource.ocid: [Entity (OCID)] Use this variable to control access to sessions targeting a specific OCI resource (for example an instance).

container_registry.target.repo.name: [String] Use this variable to control access to specific Container Registry repositories by name.

core.target.boot-volume.kms-key.id: [String] Controls whether Compute instances can be launched with boot volumes created without a Vault master encryption key.
core.target.image.id: [Entity(OCID)] The specific image OCID allowed by the policy.

dns.target.dns-domain.name: [List[String]] Use this variable to control access to specific domain names.
dns.target.dns-record.type: [List[String]] Use this variable to control access to specific DNS record types (A, AAAA, TXT, etc.).
dns.target.dns-zone.apex-label: [String] Most significant DNS label for the target zone (e.g., zone name 'service.example.com' => 'service').
[Entity (OCID)] Use this variable to control access to the destination compartment of the DNS zone by OCID.
dns.target.dns-zone.id: [Entity (OCID)] Use this variable to control access to specific DNS zones by OCID.
dns.target.dns-zone.name: [String] Use this variable to control access to specific DNS zones by name.
dns.target.dns-zone.parent-domain: [String] Domain name of the target zone's parent zone.
dns.target.dns-zone.source-compartment.id: [Entity (OCID)] Use this variable to control access to the current compartment of the DNS zone by OCID.

general.request.ad: [String] The availability domain name the request is made in (see ListAvailabilityDomains).
general.request.groups.id: [List[Entity(OCID)]] The OCIDs of the groups the requesting user is in.
general.request.instance.compartment.id: [Entity(OCID)] Compartment OCID of the requested instance.
general.request.networkSource.name: [String] The name of the network source group that specifies allowed source IPs.
general.request.operation: [String] The API operation name being requested (e.g., ListUsers).
general.request.principal.compartment.tag: [String] Tags applied to the compartment that the requesting resource belongs to.
general.request.principal.group.tag: [String] Tags applied to the groups that the requesting user belongs to.
general.request.region: [String] The 3-letter region key the request is made in (e.g., IAD, PHX, ORD, LHR, ...).
general.request.user.id: [Entity(OCID)] The OCID of the requesting user.
general.request.user.mfaTotpVerified: [Boolean] Whether the requesting user has been verified by MFA (TOTP).
general.request.user.name: [String] Name of the requesting user.
general.request.utc-timestamp: [String] UTC time the request is submitted (ISO 8601).
general.request.utc-timestamp.day-of-month: [String] Day-of-month the request is submitted in, numeric '1'..'31'.
general.request.utc-timestamp.day-of-week: [String] Day-of-week the request is submitted in English (Monday..Sunday).
general.request.utc-timestamp.month-of-year: [String] Month-of-year the request is submitted in, numeric '1'..'12'.
general.request.utc-timestamp.time-of-day: [String] UTC time interval the request is submitted during (ISO 8601 time(s), e.g. '01:00:00Z').
general.target.compartment.id: [Entity(OCID)] OCID of the compartment containing the primary resource.
general.target.compartment.name: [String] Name of the compartment specified in target.compartment.id.
general.target.resource.compartment.tag: [String] Tag applied to the target compartment of the request.
general.target.resource.tag: [String] Tag applied to the target resource of the request.

iam.target.credential.type: [String] The credential type being targeted (for example smtp/api-key/db-credential).
iam.target.domain.name: [String] Identity domain name.
iam.target.dynamic-group.id: [Entity(OCID)] The OCID of the target dynamic-group.
iam.target.dynamic-group.name: [String] The name/display of the target dynamic-group.
iam.target.group.id: [Entity(OCID)] The OCID of the target group.
iam.target.group.member: [Boolean] True if request.user is a member of target.group.
iam.target.group.name: [String] The name/display of the target group.
iam.target.policy.id: [Entity(OCID)] The OCID of the target policy.
iam.target.policy.name: [String] The name of the target policy.
iam.target.resource.domain.id: [Entity(OCID)] Identity domain OCID for the target resource.
iam.target.resource.domain.name: [String] Identity domain name for the target resource.
iam.target.tag-namespace.name: [String] Tag namespace name.
iam.target.user.id: [Entity(OCID)] The OCID of the target user.
iam.target.user.name: [String] The display/name of the target user.

orm.target.job.operation: [String] Use this variable to control access for running specified job types. For example, to limit access to PLAN and APPLY jobs, use the following phrase: where any {target.job.operation = 'PLAN', target.job.operation = 'APPLY'}
orm.target.stack.id: [Entity (OCID)] Use this variable to limit access to specified stacks. For example, use the following phrase: where any {target.stack.id = ocid1.ormstack.uniqueid1, target.stack.id = ocid1.ormstack.uniqueid2}

secure_desktops.target.desktop.id: [Entity (OCID)] Use this variable to control access to specific desktops by OCID.
secure_desktops.target.desktopPool.id: [Entity (OCID)] Use this variable to control access to a specific desktop pool by OCID.
secure_desktops.target.desktoppool.id: [Entity (OCID)] Lower-case alias of target.desktopPool.id for compatibility with policy examples.
secure_desktops.target.desktoppool.name: [String] Use this variable to control access to desktop pools by display name.

vault.target.key.id: [Entity (OCID)] Use this variable to control access to specific keys by OCID.
vault.target.secret.id: [Entity (OCID)] Use this variable to control access to specific secrets, secret versions, and secret bundles by OCID.
vault.target.secret.name: [String] Use this variable to control access to specific secrets, secret versions, and secret bundles by name.
vault.target.vault.id: [Entity (OCID)] Use this variable to control access to specific vaults by OCID.
